content_id:
Paruh kedua Sharh al-Maqasid mencakup kenabian, doktrin-doktrin eskatologis, dan Imamah - tiga area topik utama di luar sifat-sifat ilahi yang dibahas teologi kalam klasik secara sistematis. Pembahasan at-Taftazani tentang area-area ini mencerminkan baik posisi-posisi kalam standar maupun penekanan-penekanan khusus tradisi Maturidi.
Mengenai kenabian, at-Taftazani mengikuti kerangka kalam yang sudah mapan: kenabian secara rasional mungkin, realitasnya dikonfirmasi oleh mukjizat-mukjizat, dan kefi nalan kenabian Muhammad (khatm an-nubuwwah) ditetapkan oleh bukti tekstual dan rasional yang sangat kuat. Ia terlibat dengan pertanyaan tentang perlindungan para nabi dari dosa (isma) dalam bentuk Sunninya: para nabi dilindungi dari dosa-dosa besar dan dari penipuan yang disengaja, tetapi mereka dapat membuat kesalahan dalam persoalan ijtihad (interpretasi yurisprudensi) dan dalam urusan-urusan duniawi sebelum kenabian dimulai. Ini adalah posisi yang dikalibrasi dengan cermat yang membedakan doktrin Sunni tentang perlindungan kenabian dari doktrin Syiah tentang kemaksuman (yang meluas hingga para Imam) sambil tetap menetapkan keandalan komunikasi kenabian.
Mengenai eskatologi, at-Taftazani menegaskan semua doktrin utama kehidupan akhirat sebagaimana ditemukan dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah: realitas siksa dan nikmat di alam kubur, kebangkitan jasad, pengumpulan di Hari Kiamat, pertanyaan, penimbangan amal, jembatan (sirat), syafaat Nabi, surga, dan neraka. Ia terlibat dengan keberatan-keberatan filosofis terhadap kebangkitan jasad dan memberikan respons-respons kalam. Ia juga membahas pertanyaan apakah kenikmatan surga itu abadi dan apakah siksaan neraka itu abadi - posisi Sunni standar menegaskan keduanya, terhadap posisi-posisi Mu'tazilah dan mistis tertentu yang berpendapat bahwa rahmat ilahi pada akhirnya akan menyelamatkan bahkan pelaku kejahatan yang paling gigih sekalipun.
Bagian tentang Imamah dalam Sharh al-Maqasid terperinci dan mencerminkan hubungan erat tradisi Maturidi dengan mazhab Hanafi dan legitimasi Abbasiyah. At-Taftazani membela posisi Sunni standar tentang legitimasi empat khalifah pertama - Abu Bakar, Umar, Utsman, dan Ali - dan membahas keberatan-keberatan Syiah terhadap tiga yang pertama serta keberatan-keberatan Khawarij terhadap semuanya. Ia menyajikan kualifikasi-kualifikasi untuk Imamah dan membahas mekanisme-mekanisme pembentukannya, mengikuti tradisi teologi politik Sunni yang telah berkembang dengan baik.
Komprehensivitas cakupan dalam Sharh al-Maqasid - bergerak dari logika dan epistemologi melalui seluruh spektrum topik teologis hingga teologi politik - adalah yang menjadikannya referensi yang sangat berharga. Para pelajar yang mengerjakan teks tersebut menemui seluruh lanskap kalam Islam klasik dalam bentuk Maturidinya.
content_en:
The second half of Sharh al-Maqasid covers prophethood, eschatological doctrines, and the Imamate — the three major topic areas beyond the divine attributes that classical kalam theology addressed systematically. At-Taftazani's treatment of these areas reflects both the standard kalam positions and the specific emphases of the Maturidi tradition.

On prophethood, at-Taftazani follows the established kalam framework: prophethood is rationally possible, its reality is confirmed by miracles, and the finality of Muhammad's prophethood (khatm an-nubuwwah) is established by overwhelming textual and rational evidence. He engages with the question of the preservation of prophets from sin (isma) in its Sunni form: prophets are preserved from major sins and from deliberate deception, but they may make mistakes in matters of ijtihad (juristic interpretation) and in worldly affairs before prophethood begins. This is a carefully calibrated position that distinguishes the Sunni doctrine of prophetic preservation from the Shia doctrine of infallibility (which extends to the Imams) while still establishing the reliability of prophetic communication.

On eschatology, at-Taftazani affirms all the major doctrines of the afterlife as found in the Quran and Sunnah: the reality of punishment and blessing in the grave, the resurrection of bodies, the gathering on the Day of Judgment, the questioning, the weighing of deeds, the bridge (sirat), the intercession of the Prophet, paradise, and hell. He engages with philosophical objections to the resurrection of bodies and provides kalam responses. He also addresses the question of whether the bliss of paradise is eternal and whether the punishment of hell is eternal — the standard Sunni position affirms both, against certain Mutazili and mystical positions that held that divine mercy would ultimately save even the most persistent evildoers.

The section on the Imamate in Sharh al-Maqasid is detailed and reflects the Maturidi tradition's close connection to the Hanafi school and Abbasid legitimacy. At-Taftazani defends the standard Sunni position on the legitimacy of the first four caliphs — Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali — and addresses both Shia objections to the first three and Khariji objections to all of them. He presents the qualifications for the Imamate and discusses the mechanisms of its establishment, following the well-developed tradition of Sunni political theology.

The comprehensiveness of the coverage in Sharh al-Maqasid — moving from logic and epistemology through the full range of theological topics to political theology — is what makes it such a valuable reference. Students who work through the text encounter the full landscape of classical Islamic kalam in its Maturidi form.
content_ar: The ثانياً half of Sharh al-Maqasid covers prophethood, eschatological doctrines, and the Imamate — the three major topic areas beyond the divine attributes that الكلاسيكي علم الكلام theology addressed systematically. At-Taftazani's treatment of these areas reflects both the standard علم الكلام positions and the specific emphases of the الماتريدي التراث. On prophethood, at-Taftazani follows the established علم الكلام framework: prophethood is rationally possible, its reality is confirmed by miracles, and the finality of محمد ﷺ's prophethood (khatm an-nubuwwah) is established by overwhelming textual and rational evidence. He engages with the question of the preservation of الأنبياء from الذنب (isma) in its Sunni form: الأنبياء are preserved from major الذنوب and from deliberate deception, but they may make mistakes in matters of الاجتهاد (juristic interpretation) and in worldly affairs قبل prophethood begins. This is a carefully calibrated position that distinguishes the Sunni doctrine of prophetic preservation from the Shia doctrine of infallibility (which extends to the Imams) بينما still establishing the reliability of prophetic communication. On eschatology, at-Taftazani affirms all the major doctrines of the الآخرة as found in the القرآن الكريم and السنة: the reality of العقاب and البركة in the القبر, the البعث of bodies, the gathering on the يوم القيامة, the questioning, the weighing of deeds, the bridge (الصراط), the intercession of النبي ﷺ, الجنة, and النار. He engages with philosophical objections to the البعث of bodies and provides علم الكلام responses. He أيضاً addresses the question of whether the bliss of الجنة is eternal and whether the العقاب of النار is eternal — the standard Sunni position affirms both, against certain Mutazili and mystical positions that held that divine الرحمة would ultimately save even the most persistent evildoers. The الفصل on the Imamate in Sharh al-Maqasid is detailed and reflects the الماتريدي التراث's close connection to the الحنفي school and Abbasid legitimacy. At-Taftazani defends the standard Sunni position on the legitimacy of the أولاً four caliphs — أبو بكر, عمر, عثمان, and علي — and addresses both Shia objections to the أولاً three and Khariji objections to all of them. He presents the qualifications for the Imamate and discusses the mechanisms of its establishment, following the well-developed التراث of Sunni political theology. The comprehensiveness of the coverage in Sharh al-Maqasid — moving from logic and epistemology through the full range of theological topics to political theology — is what makes it such a valuable reference. Students who work through the text encounter the full landscape of الكلاسيكي Islamic علم الكلام in its الماتريدي form.
